page: Microsoft Azure G3 is a G-family virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 112.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 14.0 GB per core. The instance includes 1573 GB of bundled local storage, but does not feature any GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks reveal a mix of average and weak results, reflecting the older CPU generation. Single-core CPU performance is poor, with a stress-ng score of 870.18, and multi-core performance is weak at 5489.29. However, memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is average at 574.27 GB/sec, though large-block uncached read and write speeds drop to weak and poor levels respectively. Geekbench multi-core scores (4846.0) and Passmark CPU scores (8575.09) sit in the average tier, alongside stable Redis SET operations (9.45 million operations/sec) and static web serving throughput (7.99 GB/sec).

The primary tradeoff of the G3 instance is its low compute efficiency relative to modern processors, as evidenced by poor performance in medium-model LLM inference. However, the high memory density and substantial 1573 GB local storage capacity offer qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require large memory footprints and local data caching without demanding high-frequency CPU cycles. This server is best suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, caching layers, and general-purpose workloads requiring significant local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure G3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it delivers 112.0 GB of RAM (14.0 GB per core) and includes 1573 GB of local storage, with no GPU capabilities. Benchmark data indicates average performance in database operations, Redis SET tasks (9.45 million operations/sec), and static web serving (7.99 GB/sec), though single-core and multi-core CPU compute tests rank in the lower percentiles. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy and storage-reliant workloads rather than compute-intensive tasks. The G3 is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure G3 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 112 GB of RAM, and 1573 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure G3 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 112 GB RAM, and 1573 GB local storage, built for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine combining high memory capacity and substantial local storage for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.

112.0 GB of system memory (14.0 GB per core)
